title: Dryopteris dissidens
taxonomy: Dryopteris dissidens is classified in the kingdom Plantae, division Polypodiophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which consists of several species of ferns. The species Dryopteris dissidens is also known as the forked wood fern or the forked polypody. This classification indicates its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same group. The classification of Dryopteris dissidens reflects its position within the plant kingdom and its unique features as a species.

intro: Dryopteris dissidens, also known as the Forked Lady Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris dissidens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional Chinese medicine and folk herbalism to address respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
